title: cuscuta funiformis
intro: Cuscuta funiformis, also known as dodder, is a parasitic plant that grows by attaching itself to its host plant, obtaining nutrients through a network of hair-like structures called haustoria. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta funiformis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cough and cold symptoms, and support the immune system. Cuscuta funiformis is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, to promote overall health and well-being.
